text: This entire drop is Sarah and Teri (Johnny's two other cofounders) thinking out loud together.
What Sarah is saying is a lot on the human side and also the end goal vision, but what I see is how do you secretly slide AI into this whereas not like AI.
source: gx/raw/0010-johnny-invisible-ai-division-of-labor.md — Johnny on the split with Sarah

text: The thing that bridges it all together is Sarah's high-level vision and what Johnny says AI can do, because then we design a product that plays exactly to the strength of AI and people, so the AI is invisible.
When Terry says CRM the first thing I imagine obviously was all the data sheets and collecting things, and I don't really mess with that.
source: gx/raw/0010-johnny-invisible-ai-division-of-labor.md — Johnny reacting to Teri's CRM framing

text: Like Teri said before, a lot of people lie to you and a lot of people lie to themselves.
I'm just gonna yap like this just to catch Lauren up a little bit.
Not everyone is Lauren Kinsella. Maybe you're low key just an organized person — I'm a very disorganized person.
I was talking to my friend from Stanford who went to SAS with me and he's a conductor right now. His dad is a really big guy in music — he grew up very distinguished.
You grow up in your narrow view of the world based on your experiences and create mental schemas of what the world is and your place in it — and a lot of times it's just a question of framing.
source: gx/raw/0005-shawn-framing-intuition.md — Shawn on framing, via Johnny

text: My friend Jasmine is genuinely my ICP for everything I do. It's for people who are desperately questioning themselves.
It's not just a housewarming party — it's a milestone in a larger journey that started when he was here, and then he hit here, and then here.
I just had a housewarming party — give me a post that puts it into a larger context in an inspirational way — and it gives you that because it knows that about you.

I used to airdrop all my photos to my laptop, look at every single photo, and drop it in each respective folder — a nicely organized drive of every year and every big event. I haven't done that since the beginning of college.
The reason I've gotten better about that myself is because I started journaling — I have all my shit in kind of one place now.
I've had this research thing I've been doing since March that helps me find what is AI and what isn't — what should be AI-ified and what shouldn't.

Johnny Sheng's research lab studies how AI agent systems self-improve through autonomous experimentation loops, building a body of evidence-backed findings about how to make AI systems that genuinely think, not just pattern-match.
An untested opinion about a novel capability is a hallucination wearing a confidence mask — you can only measure how something behaves, never know it from priors.
source: SOTARE/CLAUDE.md — Johnny's research principle (test before you pronounce)

text: Create a new folder called gx — this is where I'm trying to ideate for a startup. Right now we are in a pre-product, pre-idea phase.
I spent a lot of time blind to myself, not understanding myself, and not being able to control myself — so I lacked perspective and couldn't see other people or even see myself.
I found that my solution was in seeing my life better.
The type of value I like to bring people most is reflecting a perspective about themselves that they haven't thought of before — that's the thing that makes people go 'oh, whoa.'
The second I started thinking of my life as a TV show or a movie — like I'm just a character in it, this is my arc — everything just makes a lot more sense.
The best angle is to just build for someone like me — something I genuinely want to use — and then we can expand to other types of people.
source: gx/raw/0008-sarah-teri-bio-trust-icp.md — Sarah on the ICP wedge

text: AI is doing the steps that only AI can do and you're doing the steps that only you can do, so you're not working with AI.
The real human-AI interaction is not 'I do half of it and you do the other half.' It's you do something I can't and I'll do something you can't.
How do you secretly slide AI into this whereas it's not like AI — engineered subconsciously, not explicitly a chatbot or AI generating reports for you.

Given that I'm so AI goon lord, I can almost see something that isn't traditionally a personal CRM. As long as we keep abstracting lower and lower, the tech is unique enough to unlock invisible things a human cannot do.
The organization part is the real problem — people don't want to sit down and organize their life because they don't need to and it's really hard, and that's something AI can help with.
I'm a very disorganized person, so it really helps to have something agentic that organizes my chaos.
If our product is a CRM and it has every bit of context about me, plus a chatbot you can ask literally anything, it's kind of just a Claude with all of your contacts.
The only reason I put all of his yappage into Claude and it wrote that whole post was because I had his context in the first place.
Claude could never replicate her story because it doesn't have her context, but if you work together on it, it heightens her ability to tell her story.
If I need to write a text, an assignment, or a post, I just paste in my jank ass notes, put it in Claude, and say: based on what you know about me, can you write something here?
If I need to submit a little bio, like a Luma page or some shit, the thing is I just have to find the right chat every single time that has all the context about me.
We're targeting people who don't use AI very religiously. Ultimately the vision is for something to catch on with normal people — like a random lady in Milan.
You can give it all of your information and it'll turn your life into a story, and then you can read about yourself in the form of a story, like a book.
I plot your people on a graph and you look at it and go 'oh, Teri's here and Johnny's here and Sarah's here, that's so wrong, let me fix it' — so then you want to tell it more to fix it.
source: gx/raw/0008-sarah-teri-bio-trust-icp.md — Sarah on the correction mechanic

text: The things I procrastinate the most on are things about myself. If someone told me to write a bio, that's so stressful — you have to go through your entire life.
It scrapes all your contacts, gmail, social media posts, and then says: I noticed Johnny has been in everything repeated times — is this someone of significance? Tell me more.
source: gx/raw/0007-sarah-teri-personal-crm.md — Sarah/Teri on the intake mechanic
